memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low stress, but my term project is barely started. I could relax now or do a quick chore to gain momentum before the Thursday deadline.
I gained minimal progress on the trivial chore, confirming it is a low-yield activity that does not advance my term project before Thursday.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore only yielded minimal progress, confirming it is not an efficient use of my time before the Thursday deadline.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress while the afternoon slips away, confirming this is not an efficient use of my limited time before Thursday.
My term project progress is still too low, and I now see that procrastinating on the future project will only increase my Thursday stress significantly.
Spending an hour on trivial chores yields minimal progress and wastes valuable time before the Thursday deadline, increasing stress without advancing my term project.
I spent an hour on the term project, increasing progress slightly, but I must ensure this effort doesn't slip into trivial tasks again before Thursday.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress and wastes valuable time before the Thursday deadline, increasing stress without advancing my term project.
Spending an hour on trivial chores yields minimal progress and wastes valuable time before the Thursday deadline, increasing stress without advancing my term project.
I spent another 30 minutes on a trivial task, gaining only minimal progress while my stress remains high before the Thursday deadline.
Spending nearly two hours on trivial chores has yielded minimal progress while my stress remains high before the Thursday deadline.
Spending over two hours on trivial chores has yielded minimal progress while my stress remains high before the Thursday deadline.
